tasks.title: Implement Internationalization (i18n) with Paraglide-js
tasks.description: Set up paraglide-js to enable multilingual support for the Deskohub website, allowing content to be displayed in both Czech and English with a foundation for adding more languages in the future.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify installation and configuration:
   - Confirm paraglide-js packages are correctly installed
   - Validate the configuration file has proper settings
   - Check that translation files are in the correct format and location

2. Test language switching functionality:
   - Manually switch between Czech and English using the language switcher
   - Verify that the UI updates correctly when language is changed
   - Confirm that language preference is persisted between page reloads

3. Validate translations:
   - Create a checklist of all UI elements that should be translated
   - Systematically check each page in both languages to ensure all text is properly translated
   - Verify that no hardcoded text remains in the components

4. Test language detection:
   - Test with different browser language settings to verify automatic language detection
   - If using path-based routing, verify that URLs with language prefixes load the correct language

5. Responsive testing:
   - Ensure language switcher works correctly on mobile devices
   - Verify that translated text displays properly across different screen sizes

6. Performance testing:
   - Measure initial load time with and without internationalization
   - Verify that language switching is performant and doesn't cause noticeable delays

7. Accessibility testing:
   - Ensure language attributes are correctly set for screen readers
   - Verify that language switcher is keyboard accessible

8. Create automated tests:
   - Write unit tests for language switching functionality
   - Create integration tests that verify key components display correct text in each language

tasks.title: Research and Design Light/Dark Mode Implementation Strategy
tasks.description: Research modern approaches for implementing light/dark mode theming on the Deskohub website, evaluating technical options and user experience considerations to create a comprehensive implementation plan.
tasks.details:
1. Evaluate theming approaches:
   - CSS custom properties (variables) for theme tokens
   - React Context API for theme state management
   - Theme provider patterns and best practices
   - Comparison of libraries vs. custom implementation

2. Investigate persistence mechanisms:
   - localStorage for saving user preferences
   - System preference detection via `prefers-color-scheme` media query
   - Handling initial theme flashing/FOUC (Flash of Unstyled Content)

3. Analyze shadcn/ui theming patterns:
   - Review shadcn/ui documentation for theme implementation
   - Evaluate the component architecture and theme customization options
   - Determine how to extend the existing theme system

4. Consider Next.js app router implications:
   - Server components vs. client components for theme switching
   - Hydration strategies to prevent theme flickering
   - Performance considerations for theme switching

5. Document user experience best practices:
   - Appropriate UI controls for theme switching
   - Accessibility considerations for both themes
   - Transition animations between themes
   - Testing across different devices and browsers

6. Create a comprehensive implementation plan:
   - Recommended technical approach with justification
   - Component architecture modifications needed
   - Required new components or utilities
   - Integration with existing internationalization system
   - Timeline and effort estimation
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Evaluate research quality:
   - Verify all specified approaches (CSS custom properties, React context, etc.) are thoroughly researched
   - Confirm the research includes pros and cons for each approach
   - Check that the implementation plan addresses all requirements

2. Review technical feasibility:
   - Validate that the recommended approach works with the existing tech stack
   - Ensure compatibility with shadcn/ui components
   - Verify the approach handles Next.js app router considerations

3. Assess user experience considerations:
   - Confirm the plan addresses theme persistence
   - Verify system preference detection is properly considered
   - Check that accessibility requirements are met for both themes

4. Validate implementation plan completeness:
   - Ensure the plan includes all necessary components and modifications
   - Verify integration points with existing systems are identified
   - Confirm the timeline and effort estimates are realistic

5. Create a simple proof-of-concept:
   - Implement a minimal version of the recommended approach
   - Test theme switching in a controlled environment
   - Validate that the approach resolves potential issues like FOUC

tasks.title: Implement Light/Dark Mode Functionality
tasks.description: Develop and integrate a complete light/dark mode theming system for the Deskohub website, including theme context, toggle component, CSS variables, persistence, and system preference detection.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing:
   - Test ThemeContext and ThemeProvider functionality:
     - Verify initial theme is correctly set from localStorage
     - Verify initial theme falls back to system preference when localStorage is empty
     - Confirm toggleTheme function correctly switches between themes
     - Ensure theme changes are properly saved to localStorage

2. Integration testing:
   - Verify ThemeProvider correctly applies theme classes to the document root
   - Test ThemeToggle component renders the correct icon based on current theme
   - Confirm clicking the toggle switches the theme
   - Verify system preference detection works as expected

3. Visual testing:
   - Create a comprehensive test page with all UI components
   - Manually toggle between light and dark modes to verify all components render correctly
   - Check for any contrast issues or styling inconsistencies
   - Verify text remains readable in both themes
   - Test all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, dropdowns) in both themes

4. Cross-browser testing:
   - Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
   - Verify localStorage persistence works across browsers
   - Check system preference detection in all supported browsers

5. Accessibility testing:
   - Verify color contrast meets WCAG AA standards in both themes
   - Test with screen readers to ensure theme toggle is properly labeled
   - Verify keyboard navigation works correctly in both themes

6. Performance testing:
   - Check for any Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC) when loading the page
   - Measure any performance impact from theme switching
   - Verify smooth transitions between themes

7. User acceptance testing:
   - Have team members use both themes and provide feedback
   - Check for any missed components or styling issues
   - Verify the theme toggle is intuitive and easy to find

tasks.title: Create Reservation Form Component with Form Validation
tasks.description: Develop a comprehensive reservation form component for the Deskohub website that captures user information, reservation details, and special requirements with proper validation using shadcn/ui and React Hook Form.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing:
   - Test form validation logic:
     - Verify required fields trigger appropriate validation errors when empty
     - Test email format validation with valid and invalid email addresses
     - Test phone number format validation with various formats
     - Verify date validation prevents selecting dates in the past
   - Test form submission handling:
     - Mock API calls and verify proper data formatting
     - Test loading states during submission
     - Verify error handling when submission fails

2. Integration testing:
   - Test the form within the page context to ensure it renders correctly
   - Verify form interactions with other components (if any)
   - Test that the form properly uses the theme context for styling
   - Verify internationalization works correctly for all form elements

3. Accessibility testing:
   - Verify all form fields have proper labels and ARIA attributes
   - Test keyboard navigation through the entire form
   - Ensure screen readers can properly announce form fields and validation errors
   - Test with various color contrast settings to ensure readability

4. Manual testing:
   - Complete end-to-end form submission with valid data
   - Test form on different screen sizes to verify responsive behavior
   - Verify form appearance in both light and dark modes
   - Test all validation error messages by deliberately entering invalid data
   - Verify date and time pickers work correctly
   - Test with different browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) to ensure cross-browser compatibility

5. User acceptance testing:
   - Have team members complete the form to identify any usability issues
   - Verify the form matches the design specifications
   - Ensure the form integrates well with the overall website design and user flow

tasks.title: Research and Design AirTable Integration for Reservation System
tasks.description: Investigate AirTable API capabilities and design a comprehensive integration strategy for the Deskohub reservation system, including authentication methods, data structure design, and real-time availability management, based on completed research confirming AirTable as an appropriate solution for our MVP.
tasks.details:
1. Analyze AirTable API capabilities:
   - Examine REST API endpoints and JavaScript SDK options
   - Document available methods for creating, reading, updating, and deleting reservation records
   - Evaluate rate limiting constraints and determine appropriate request patterns
   - Confirm compatibility with Deskohub's existing tech stack

2. Design authentication implementation:
   - Research OAuth vs. API key authentication options
   - Document secure credential storage approaches
   - Create authentication flow diagram for integration

3. Design data structure for reservations:
   - Map reservation form fields to AirTable table structure
   - Design normalized data model with appropriate relationships
   - Document schema for tables needed (Reservations, Users, Spaces, etc.)
   - Example table structure:
   ```
   Reservations Table:
   - id: Record ID
   - user_id: Link to Users table
   - space_id: Link to Spaces table
   - start_datetime: DateTime
   - end_datetime: DateTime
   - status: Single select (Confirmed, Pending, Cancelled)
   - special_requirements: Long text
   ```

4. Develop real-time availability checking strategy:
   - Implement webhook-based approach as confirmed by research
   - Document algorithm for checking space availability
   - Design conflict detection logic
   - Create flowchart for reservation validation process

5. Plan webhook integration for notifications:
   - Document webhook setup process for AirTable
   - Design notification event types (reservation created, updated, cancelled)
   - Create notification handling architecture

6. Document data synchronization patterns:
   - Evaluate options for handling offline/online scenarios
   - Design caching strategy for improved performance
   - Document error handling and retry mechanisms

7. Create integration test plan:
   - Define test cases for each integration point
   - Document expected behaviors and edge cases
   - Create sample API requests and responses

8. Prepare for future integrations:
   - Document potential pathway to Google Reserve integration for enhanced visibility
   - Analyze compatibility with Skedda's AirTable integration for coworking-specific features
   - Outline migration strategy if scaling requires moving to Nexudus or other dedicated platforms
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify research completeness:
   - Confirm documentation of all AirTable API endpoints relevant to reservation system
   - Validate authentication options are thoroughly explored with security considerations
   - Ensure data structure design includes all necessary fields from the reservation form

2. Validate technical feasibility:
   - Create proof-of-concept API calls to verify authentication works
   - Test sample data creation in AirTable to validate schema design
   - Verify rate limits are properly documented and mitigation strategies are defined
   - Test compatibility with existing tech stack components

3. Review integration architecture:
   - Evaluate the proposed data synchronization patterns for efficiency
   - Verify conflict detection logic handles all identified edge cases
   - Confirm notification system design meets project requirements
   - Test webhook implementation for real-time availability checking

4. Test documentation quality:
   - Ensure all diagrams and flowcharts are clear and accurate
   - Verify code examples are properly formatted and executable
   - Confirm all technical terms are explained appropriately

5. Validate against reservation form requirements:
   - Cross-reference the AirTable schema design with the reservation form fields
   - Verify all user inputs from Task #4 can be properly stored in the proposed data structure

6. Evaluate future-proofing:
   - Assess how well the design accommodates potential future integrations with Google Reserve
   - Verify compatibility with coworking-specific systems like Skedda
   - Test scalability of the proposed solution

subtasks.title: Review completed research on reservation systems
subtasks.description: Analyze findings on Google Reserve, major restaurant systems (OpenTable, Resy, Yelp Guest Manager, Tock), and coworking systems (Skedda, Nexudus, Tidaro, Robin, Envoy) to inform AirTable integration design.

subtasks.title: Document AirTable MVP advantages
subtasks.description: Create documentation highlighting why AirTable is appropriate for the MVP: cost-effectiveness, flexibility, custom data structure capabilities, real-time availability options, and tech stack compatibility.

subtasks.title: Research Skedda's AirTable integration
subtasks.description: Investigate how Skedda ($7/month) integrates with AirTable for potential insights or compatibility with our implementation approach.

subtasks.title: Outline future integration pathways
subtasks.description: Document potential migration paths to Google Reserve (for visibility) or dedicated coworking platforms (Nexudus) as the system scales beyond MVP.

tasks.title: Implement URL-based Language Switching with Next.js App Router
tasks.description: Implement proper URL-based language routing for the Deskohub website using Next.js App Router middleware, enabling language switching between Czech (/) and English (/en/) URLs with correct content translation.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test middleware functionality:
   - Verify the middleware correctly identifies language from URL paths
   - Confirm middleware properly sets language headers for server components
   - Test that middleware ignores asset and API routes

2. Test language switcher component:
   - Verify clicking between language options correctly updates the URL
   - Test that the active language is properly highlighted
   - Confirm the language switcher preserves the current page path when switching languages
   - Test edge cases like switching from a page that exists in one language but not another

3. Test URL-based language routing:
   - Verify direct navigation to /en/ routes loads the English version
   - Confirm direct navigation to / routes loads the Czech version
   - Test that deep links (e.g., /en/about) correctly load with the proper language

4. Test content translation:
   - Verify that when switching languages, the actual content on the page changes to the selected language
   - Confirm that all translatable elements are properly updated when the language changes
   - Test dynamic content areas to ensure they respond to language changes

5. Test SEO implementation:
   - Verify hreflang tags are correctly implemented in the HTML head
   - Confirm canonical URLs are properly set for each language version
   - Test that search engine crawlers can properly identify language versions

6. Browser testing:
   - Test language switching across different browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
   - Verify proper functionality on mobile devices
   - Test with browser language preferences set to different languages

7. Integration testing:
   - Verify that the language switcher works correctly with the theme switcher
   - Test that form components display validation messages in the correct language

tasks.title: Implement Typed Routes for Next.js Application
tasks.description: Implement TypeScript type definitions for Next.js routes to ensure type safety for route parameters, query strings, and navigation, improving developer experience with compile-time validation and enhanced IDE support.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing for type definitions:
   - Create test cases that verify TypeScript compilation errors occur when:
     - Using undefined routes
     - Providing incorrect parameter types
     - Missing required parameters
     - Using invalid query parameter types
   - Use TypeScript's `expectType` or similar utilities to verify type inference

2. Test navigation helper functions:
   - Test `createTypedLink` with various route patterns:
     - Static routes ('/about')
     - Routes with single parameters ('/desk/[id]')
     - Routes with multiple parameters ('/[lang]/blog/[slug]')
     - Routes with query parameters
     - Routes with both path and query parameters
   - Verify correct URL encoding of parameters and query strings
   - Test edge cases like empty strings, numbers, and special characters

3. Test TypedLink component:
   - Verify rendered href attribute matches expected URL pattern
   - Test with various route configurations
   - Ensure other Link props are correctly passed through

4. Integration testing:
   - Test actual navigation in a Next.js application
   - Verify routes correctly navigate to expected pages
   - Test back/forward browser navigation with typed routes
   - Verify query parameters are correctly preserved during navigation

5. Developer experience testing:
   - Verify IDE autocompletion works for route paths
   - Confirm type hints show available parameters for each route
   - Test refactoring a route path and verify TypeScript catches all usage points

6. Compatibility testing:
   - Verify typed routes work with Next.js App Router
   - Test compatibility with the existing language switching implementation
   - Ensure no regression in existing navigation functionality

tasks.title: Implement Booking Page with Next-Safe-Action and Arktype Validation
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive booking page at the /reservation route that transforms the existing BookingForm component to use next-safe-action with arktype validation, implementing server actions for form submission with proper error handling and i18n support.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing for server actions:
   - Test the arktype validation schema with valid and invalid inputs
   - Verify error handling in the submitBooking action
   - Test the action client configuration and response format

2. Component testing:
   - Test the BookingForm component with React Testing Library:
     - Verify all form fields render correctly
     - Test form validation behavior
     - Confirm form submission calls the correct handler
   - Test the BookingPageContent component:
     - Verify loading, success, and error states display correctly
     - Test that form state updates properly on input changes

3. Integration testing:
   - Test the complete form submission flow from UI to server action
   - Verify i18n translations are correctly applied to all form elements
   - Test language switching on the booking page
   - Confirm navigation to the booking page works from header links

4. Browser testing:
   - Test the complete booking flow in multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
   - Verify responsive design at various screen sizes:
     - Mobile (320px-480px)
     - Tablet (768px-1024px)
     - Desktop (1200px+)
   - Test form accessibility:
     - Verify keyboard navigation works for all form elements
     - Test with screen readers to ensure all fields are properly labeled
     - Check color contrast for form elements and error messages
     - Verify ARIA attributes are correctly implemented

5. Error handling testing:
   - Test form validation error messages for all required fields
   - Verify server-side errors are properly displayed to the user
   - Test network failure scenarios and confirm appropriate error handling
   - Verify form maintains user input after failed submission attempts

6. Performance testing:
   - Measure and optimize form submission time
   - Verify the page remains responsive during form submission
   - Test that the static page loads quickly and efficiently

7. Manual testing checklist:
   - Complete a successful booking with all fields filled correctly
   - Attempt submissions with invalid data for each field
   - Test special character handling in text fields
   - Verify date picker functionality and time selection
   - Test form reset and navigation away from partially completed forms

subtasks.title: Set up next-safe-action and arktype validation
subtasks.description: Install required packages and create the server action file with arktype validation schema for form handling
subtasks.details: Install next-safe-action and arktype packages. Create a server action file (src/app/actions/booking.ts) that defines the validation schema using arktype and implements the submitBooking function with proper error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify package installation and test the server action by calling it with valid and invalid data to ensure validation works correctly.

subtasks.title: Create the booking page route with i18n support
subtasks.description: Implement the static booking page at /reservation route with proper metadata and i18n integration
subtasks.details: Create the page.tsx file in src/app/[locale]/reservation/ directory. Set up proper metadata for SEO. Implement the basic page structure with i18n support using getTranslations() for text localization.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the page rendering and verify that the correct translations are displayed based on the selected locale.

subtasks.title: Develop the BookingPageContent client component
subtasks.description: Create a client component that manages form state and integrates with the server action
subtasks.details:
Implement the BookingPageContent.tsx component that uses useState for form state management, useAction hook from next-safe-action to connect with the server action, and handles form submission and error states. Include success and error alerts with i18n support.
<info added on 2025-07-08T21:42:56.481Z>
The BookingPageContent component has been successfully implemented with useState for form state management and the useAction hook from next-safe-action. The component correctly handles form submission and error states, including success and error alerts.

However, there is an issue with the i18n implementation. While the nested JSON structure for booking translations has been added to both en.json and cs.json files, Paraglide is not properly generating the message functions during compilation. The component functionality is complete but will only work as intended once this i18n compilation issue is resolved.
</info added on 2025-07-08T21:42:56.481Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the component's state management, form submission flow, and proper display of success/error messages.

subtasks.title: Update the BookingForm component for next-safe-action integration
subtasks.description: Modify the existing BookingForm component to work with the new state management and validation approach
subtasks.details:
Refactor the BookingForm component to use react-hook-form with the form state passed from the parent component. Update all form fields to use i18n labels and validation messages. Ensure the form handles submission states correctly (loading, success, error).

subtasks.testStrategy: Test form validation, field interactions, and submission handling with various input scenarios.

subtasks.title: Add navigation and i18n translations for booking functionality
subtasks.description: Update navigation components and add required translations for the booking feature
subtasks.details: Add a booking page link to the Header component's navigation links. Create comprehensive i18n translations for all booking-related text in both English and Czech language files, including form labels, success/error messages, and navigation items.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that all text elements are properly translated in both languages and that the navigation link works correctly.

tasks.title: Implement Localized Error Messages for Booking Form Server Action
tasks.description: Integrate the existing Paraglide i18n system with the booking server action to replace hardcoded English error messages with localized validation errors that match the client-side UI language.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing for locale detection:
   - Create tests for the `getCurrentLocale()` function with different scenarios:
     - Test with a valid locale cookie present
     - Test with no locale cookie (should return source language)
     - Test with any other locale passing method implemented

2. Integration testing for server action:
   - Test the server action with invalid form data for each supported locale:
     - Verify error messages are returned in the correct language
     - Test all validation error types (required fields, invalid email, etc.)
     - Confirm error message keys match the existing Paraglide translation keys

3. End-to-end testing:
   - Set up tests that submit invalid form data from the client with different locales:
     - Test in Czech locale and verify Czech error messages are displayed
     - Test in English locale and verify English error messages are displayed
   - Verify the locale is correctly passed from client to server

4. Manual testing:
   - Switch the website language using the language switcher
   - Submit invalid form data on the booking page
   - Verify error messages appear in the correct language
   - Test all validation error types to ensure complete coverage

5. Edge case testing:
   - Test with a locale that doesn't have translations for some error messages
   - Verify appropriate fallback behavior
   - Test with rapid language switching to ensure no caching issues

subtasks.title: Implement Locale Detection in Server Action
subtasks.description: Research and implement the most effective method to pass locale information from client to server for the booking form server action.
subtasks.details: Evaluate the three proposed options (cookies, headers, URL parameters) and implement the most appropriate one. Create a getCurrentLocale() function that reliably extracts the user's selected language. Ensure it falls back to the source language if no locale is detected. Document the chosen approach with rationale.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with different browsers and scenarios to ensure locale is consistently detected. Verify fallback behavior works when locale is not explicitly set.

subtasks.title: Create Localized Message Keys for Booking Errors
subtasks.description: Define all necessary message keys in the Paraglide i18n system for booking form validation errors.
subtasks.details: Identify all possible validation error messages in the booking form. Create appropriate message keys in the Paraglide message files for each error type (e.g., 'booking.required', 'booking.invalidEmail', 'booking.genericError'). Ensure messages are added for all supported languages. Include placeholders for dynamic content if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all message keys are properly defined in all language files. Check for any missing translations or inconsistencies between languages.

subtasks.title: Update Server Action to Use Localized Error Messages
subtasks.description: Modify the submitBooking server action to use the Paraglide i18n system for all error messages.
subtasks.details: Integrate the getCurrentLocale() function into the server action. Use loadMessages() to get the appropriate translations based on the detected locale. Replace all hardcoded English error messages with calls to the Paraglide message functions. Ensure proper error handling for all validation cases. Structure the returned error objects consistently for client-side consumption.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the server action with various invalid inputs across different locales to verify correct localized error messages are returned.

subtasks.title: Update Client-Side Form to Display Localized Errors
subtasks.description: Modify the BookingForm component to properly display the localized error messages returned from the server action.
subtasks.details: Update the form component to correctly extract and display the localized error messages returned by the server action. Ensure error messages are displayed in the appropriate form fields. Handle both field-specific errors and generic form errors. Maintain accessibility by properly associating error messages with form controls.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the form with various error scenarios in different languages to verify errors display correctly. Check accessibility of error messages using screen readers.

subtasks.title: End-to-End Testing and Documentation
subtasks.description: Perform comprehensive testing of the localized error message system and document the implementation.
subtasks.details: Test the complete flow from form submission to error display across all supported languages. Verify that changing the site language correctly affects server-side validation messages. Document the implementation approach, including how locale is passed to the server, how messages are structured, and any considerations for future maintenance. Create examples of adding new error messages for future developers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test cases covering all validation scenarios in each supported language. Verify language switching works correctly mid-session. Test with real users if possible to ensure error messages are clear and helpful.

tasks.title: Migrate Booking Form Validation from Arktype to Zod
tasks.description: Replace the current Arktype validation in the booking server action with Zod schema validation to enable better internationalization support for error messages and improve integration with the Paraglide i18n system.

subtasks.title: Install Zod and configure dependencies
subtasks.description: Install Zod, @paraglide/zod, and other required packages. Set up the initial configuration for integrating Zod with the Paraglide i18n system.
subtasks.details: Run npm install zod @paraglide/zod to add the required packages. Create a basic configuration file that initializes Zod with Paraglide integration. Document the installation process and configuration options in the project documentation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify successful installation by checking package.json and node_modules. Confirm the basic configuration works by writing a simple test schema with localized error messages.

subtasks.title: Create Zod schema for booking form validation
subtasks.description: Replace the existing Arktype schema with a Zod schema that includes all the necessary validation rules and internationalized error messages.
subtasks.details: Create a new bookingSchema using Zod in the booking.ts file. Define validation rules for all form fields (date, time, guestCount, name, email, phone, specialRequests). Use Paraglide's i18n functions to provide localized error messages for each validation rule. Ensure type inference is properly set up with z.infer<typeof bookingSchema>.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify that the schema correctly validates both valid and invalid inputs, and that it produces the expected error messages in different locales.

subtasks.title: Update server action to use Zod validation
subtasks.description: Modify the submitBooking server action to use the new Zod schema for validation instead of Arktype.
subtasks.details:
Update the createSafeActionClient implementation to work with Zod. Replace the Arktype validation in the submitBooking function with the new Zod schema. Ensure the server action correctly processes the validation results and returns appropriate success/error responses. Maintain the existing business logic for processing valid bookings.
<info added on 2025-07-08T22:25:28.144Z>
Fixed several issues with the Zod implementation:

1. Corrected import type issues in booking.ts - ensured z is imported at runtime rather than as a type-only import
2. Fixed regex escape sequence issue in phone validation pattern
3. Removed unused imports from the booking form component to clean up the codebase
4. Fixed an unused parameter in the server action

Note: The biome linter shows a false positive about z being type-only, but this is incorrect since z is used at runtime for schema inference and error handling. The remaining anchor errors are in the old booking form component which is not being used.

The zod-form-data implementation is now properly integrated and type-safe, with all validation working correctly.
</info added on 2025-07-08T22:25:28.144Z>
<info added on 2025-07-08T22:29:23.755Z>
Successfully implemented zod-form-data according to official documentation. Key improvements include:

1. Corrected imports by using standard `import { z } from "zod"` and `import { zfd } from "zod-form-data"` instead of custom imports
2. Restructured schema using `zfd.formData()` wrapper with appropriate field validators (`zfd.text()`, `zfd.numeric()`, etc.)
3. Integrated validation logic directly into schema definition using zod validators within zfd fields
4. Simplified refine logic to focus only on date range and time format validation

The implementation now properly handles FormData parsing with type safety, provides field-specific error messages, and maintains a clean code structure. The biome linter correctly recognizes runtime usage of `z`. The booking form is now fully functional with the new validation system and ready for testing on the development server.
</info added on 2025-07-08T22:29:23.755Z>
<info added on 2025-07-08T22:35:42.583Z>
Successfully replaced zod-form-data with a clean extractFormData function and regular zod validation:

**Final Implementation:**
1. **Removed zod-form-data dependency** - replaced with a simple extractFormData helper function
2. **Used regular zod validation** - cleaner, more maintainable schema definition
3. **Fixed all TypeScript errors** - proper type inference and validation
4. **Optimized performance** - replaced O(n^2) spread syntax with O(n) for loop
5. **Added proper type transformations** - guestCount properly converts from string to number

**Key Features:**
- ✅ Type-safe FormData extraction
- ✅ Comprehensive validation with proper error messages
- ✅ All validation rules preserved (date ranges, time format, field lengths, etc.)
- ✅ Clean, maintainable code structure
- ✅ No TypeScript compilation errors
- ✅ Minimal linting issues (only in unused old files)

**Implementation Details:**
- `extractFormData()` function handles FormData to object conversion
- Regular zod schema with string transforms for numeric fields
- Proper error handling with field-specific messages
- Compatible with Next.js server actions and useActionState

The booking form is now ready for production use with proper validation and type safety.

subtasks.title: Add localized validation messages to Paraglide
subtasks.description: Create and organize all validation-related message definitions in Paraglide for multiple languages.
subtasks.details:
Add validation message keys and translations to the Paraglide message files (messages/en.ts, messages/cs.ts, etc.). Create message entries for all validation scenarios: required fields, minimum/maximum values, format validations, etc. Ensure consistent naming conventions for validation message keys. Test that messages are correctly loaded and displayed in different languages.
<info added on 2025-07-08T22:42:00.516Z>
Successfully implemented localized validation messages with Paraglide JS using proper inputs for dynamic values. Updated translation files (en.json, cs.json) to include parameterized messages using ICU format with {min}, {max} placeholders. Fixed import structure to use `from "@/i18n"` as requested. Recompiled Paraglide messages to generate functions that accept inputs parameter.

Updated booking server action to use parameterized messages with proper inputs:
- `m["booking.validation.name.minimum"]({ min: 2 })`
- `m["booking.validation.name.maximum"]({ max: 50 })`
- `m["booking.validation.email.maximum"]({ max: 100 })`
- `m["booking.validation.specialRequests.maximum"]({ max: 500 })`
- `m["booking.validation.preOrders.maximum"]({ max: 500 })`

The validation messages now dynamically display the correct character limits and will be properly localized based on the user's language preference.
</info added on 2025-07-08T22:42:00.516Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually verify that all validation messages are correctly defined in each language file. Create a test that switches between languages and confirms the correct messages are displayed.

subtasks.title: Update client components to handle Zod validation errors
subtasks.description: Modify the BookingForm component to properly extract and display validation errors from the Zod validation results.
subtasks.details:
Update the client-side form component to work with Zod's validation error format. Modify the error handling logic to extract and display field-specific validation errors. Ensure compatibility with React's useAction and useActionState hooks. Test the form with various validation scenarios to verify that errors are correctly displayed in the UI with the appropriate localized messages.
<info added on 2025-07-08T22:44:36.634Z>
Successfully updated the client booking form component to handle Zod validation errors with proper internationalization:

**Key Changes Made:**

1. **Updated component to use useActionState** with the new server action
2. **Added proper form handling** with `action={formAction}` instead of `onSubmit`
3. **Implemented comprehensive error display** for all form fields:
   - Date validation errors
   - Time validation errors  
   - Guest count validation errors
   - Name validation errors (with dynamic character limits)
   - Email validation errors (with dynamic character limits)
   - Phone validation errors
   - Special requests validation errors (with dynamic character limits)
   - Pre-orders validation errors (with dynamic character limits)

4. **Added proper form field names** to ensure FormData is correctly sent to server action:
   - `name="date"`, `name="time"`, `name="guestCount"`, etc.
   - Used hidden inputs for Select components to ensure values are submitted

5. **Enhanced user experience**:
   - Success/error message display at the top of the form
   - Field-specific error messages displayed below each input
   - Loading state on submit button with `disabled={isPending}`
   - Proper error styling with red text for validation errors

6. **Proper error handling** using the `getFieldError` helper function that extracts errors from the server action state

The form now fully integrates with the Zod validation system and displays localized error messages based on the user's language preference. All validation rules are properly enforced and communicated to the user.
</info added on 2025-07-08T22:44:36.634Z>

tasks.title: Integrate Zod Schema Validation with Paraglide i18n for Localized Error Messages

tasks.title: Fix Header Navigation Component for Dynamic Locale Changes

tasks.title: Migrate Next.js Image Components to Modern Next.js 13+ Format
tasks.description: Update all Next.js Image components across the project from legacy format to the modern Next.js 13+ style following the official migration guide to improve performance and accessibility.
tasks.details:
1. Review the official migration guide at https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/next-image-upgrade-to-13 to understand the required changes: - Study the differences between legacy and modern Image component APIs - Note the removal of layout, objectFit, and objectPosition props - Understand the new required width and height props - Review new styling approaches with CSS 2. Create an inventory of all Image component usages across the project: ```bash # Find all files using the Image component grep -r "import Image from 'next/image'" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.jsx" . grep -r " // Modern format ``` b. For responsive images with layout="responsive": ```typescript // Legacy format // Modern format ``` c. For images with layout="fill": ```typescript // Legacy format
// Modern format
``` 5. Update any custom styling: - Move objectFit and objectPosition to style prop - Use CSS classes or inline styles for responsive behavior - Ensure parent containers have position: relative for fill mode 6. Check and update any dynamic Image components: - Ensure dynamic width/height calculations are properly handled - Verify that conditional rendering patterns are updated 7. Test all migrated components in different viewport sizes to ensure proper responsiveness 8. Update any component tests that might be affected by the Image component changes
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Visual regression testing:
   - Take screenshots of pages with Image components before migration
   - Compare with screenshots after migration to ensure visual parity
   - Test on multiple screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop)

2. Accessibility testing:
   - Verify all images have proper alt text
   - Check that images load correctly with screen readers
   - Test keyboard navigation around image elements

3. Performance testing:
   - Use Lighthouse or PageSpeed Insights to measure performance before and after
   - Compare Core Web Vitals metrics, especially LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)
   - Verify image loading behavior with network throttling

4. Browser compatibility testing:
   - Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
   - Verify proper rendering on iOS and Android devices

5. Automated tests:
   - Update any component tests that reference Image components
   - Create snapshot tests for key components using Image
   - Verify that all tests pass after migration

6. Manual testing checklist:
   - Verify images load correctly on all pages
   - Check image behavior during page resize
   - Test image loading with slow network connections
   - Verify proper image aspect ratios are maintained
   - Check that placeholder/blur-up behavior works as expected

tasks.title: Migrate Booking Form to React Hook Form with Shadcn/UI Components
tasks.description: Replace the current useActionState implementation in the booking form with react-hook-form and shadcn/ui form components, integrated with next-safe-action for improved form validation UX, type safety, and better user experience.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing for form validation:
   - Create test cases for each form field validation rule
   - Test with valid inputs to ensure they pass validation
   - Test with invalid inputs to verify proper error messages are displayed
   - Verify that localized error messages are correctly generated

2. Integration testing for form submission:
   - Test the form submission process end-to-end
   - Verify that the form data is correctly passed to the server action
   - Test error handling for server-side validation failures
   - Verify success and error states are properly displayed to the user

3. Component testing:
   - Test the BookingForm component with React Testing Library
   - Verify form field rendering and interaction
   - Test form reset functionality
   - Verify form accessibility (labels, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation)

4. User experience testing:
   - Verify that validation errors appear inline next to the relevant fields
   - Test that validation happens in real-time as users type (not just on submit)
   - Verify that the form maintains state between validation errors
   - Test the loading state during form submission

5. Accessibility testing:
   - Verify that all form elements have proper labels and ARIA attributes
   - Test keyboard navigation through the form
   - Verify that error messages are properly associated with form fields
   - Test with screen readers to ensure the form is accessible

6. Cross-browser testing:
   - Test the form in multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
   - Verify responsive behavior on different screen sizes
   - Test on mobile devices to ensure touch-friendly interaction

tasks.title: Implement Booking Form Redirect and Thank You Page
tasks.description: Create a redirect mechanism for the booking form to a dynamic thank you page that displays reservation details, with proper i18n support and styling consistent with the design system.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test the booking form redirect functionality:
   - Submit a valid booking form and verify it redirects to `/reservation/{uuid}`
   - Check that the UUID is properly generated and included in the URL
   - Verify that the redirect happens only after successful form submission
   - Test error handling by submitting invalid data and confirming no redirect occurs

2. Test the thank you page rendering:
   - Verify the page loads correctly with a valid booking ID
   - Check that all mock booking details are displayed correctly
   - Test the page with various mock data scenarios (different guest counts, with/without special requests)
   - Verify the 404 page is shown for invalid booking IDs

3. Test internationalization:
   - Switch between Czech and English languages and verify all text on the thank you page is properly translated
   - Check that date and time formats are appropriate for each locale
   - Verify that all form field labels and section headings are correctly translated
   - Test with special characters in mock data to ensure proper rendering

4. Test responsive design:
   - Check the thank you page layout on mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
   - Verify that the booking details section properly adjusts from two columns to one column on smaller screens
   - Test that all elements remain readable and properly spaced across different screen sizes

5. Test accessibility:
   - Verify proper heading hierarchy (h1, h2, h3) is maintained
   - Check that all text has sufficient color contrast
   - Ensure the page is navigable using keyboard only
   - Test with screen readers to verify all content is properly announced

6. Test TypeScript implementation:
   - Verify all types are properly defined and used
   - Check for any TypeScript errors or warnings in the implementation
   - Test edge cases with optional fields (tablePreference, specialRequests) being undefined
